title: Background Blocking Sub-Agents via Keyboard Shortcut
description: Allow users to push currently-blocking sub-agents to the background via a keyboard shortcut, freeing the parent agent while sub-agents continue running

work_packages.title: Detach controller and runtime wiring
work_packages.description: Create the detach controller module, extend the ExtensionRuntime interface, and wire the detach trigger into the TUI interrupt handler. This establishes the signal path from keyboard input to tool execution.
work_packages.rationale: The detach controller, runtime integration, and TUI key handler form a single signal path — the controller API shapes the interrupt handler integration and the runtime interface changes.

work_packages.title: Tool integration for Agent and subagent_run
work_packages.description: Modify the blocking paths in both the Agent tool and subagent_run tool to race their donePromise against the detach signal, handle partial results, and defer concurrency release for detached sub-agents.
work_packages.rationale: Both tools share the same detach integration pattern and touch the same runtime API surface. Implementing them together ensures consistent behavior and avoids divergent approaches.

work_packages.title: Unit tests for detach functionality
work_packages.description: Add unit tests covering the detach controller, tool detach behavior across all modes (single, parallel tasks, chain), concurrency tracking correctness, and interrupt handler key binding.
work_packages.rationale: Tests span the full detach feature and need both the controller and tool integration to be complete before meaningful assertions can be written. A single test task avoids artificial splits of tightly-coupled test scenarios.

phases.name: Core Infrastructure
phases.description: Create the detach signaling mechanism and wire it through the runtime and TUI layers

tasks.title: Create detach controller module
tasks.description: Create `src/runtime/detach.ts` exporting a `DetachController` that manages detach scopes. Each scope provides a Promise that resolves when the user triggers a detach. The controller exposes `createScope()`, `detachAll()`, and `hasActiveScopes()`. Scopes auto-clean on dispose to prevent leaks.
acceptance_criteria: Exported `createDetachController()` returns a `DetachController` with `createScope()`, `detachAll()`, and `hasActiveScopes()`
`createScope()` returns a `DetachScope` with a Promise, a manual `resolve()`, and `dispose()` for cleanup
`detachAll()` resolves all active scopes' promises and clears them
`dispose()` on a scope removes it from the controller without resolving its promise
Calling `detachAll()` when no scopes are active is a no-op
tasks.context: REQ-1 requires a mechanism to signal blocking tools to release their await. This controller is the coordination primitive between the TUI key handler and tool execution.

tasks.title: Wire detach controller into runtime and TUI
tasks.description: Extend the `ExtensionRuntime` interface in `src/runtime/types.ts` with a `detach` property exposing the `DetachController`. Instantiate the controller in `src/index.ts` and add it to the runtime object. Add a new key binding (Ctrl+B) to `src/ui/interrupt.ts` that calls `detachAll()` on the controller when active blocking scopes exist. Show a notification to the user when sub-agents are backgrounded.
acceptance_criteria: ExtensionRuntime has a `detach` property typed as DetachController
The controller is instantiated in index.ts and accessible via rt.detach
Ctrl+B in the TUI triggers detachAll() when hasActiveScopes() is true
Ctrl+B is consumed (not passed through) when active scopes exist
Ctrl+B passes through normally when no active scopes exist
A non-empty notification message is rendered in the TUI when detach is triggered
The new key binding does not conflict with Ctrl+C or double-Escape
tasks.context: REQ-1 and REQ-8 require a non-conflicting keyboard trigger. Ctrl+B is chosen as a mnemonic for 'background' and is not currently bound in the interrupt handler.

phases.name: Tool Integration
phases.description: Modify the blocking tool paths to race against detach signals and handle backgrounding correctly

tasks.title: Integrate detach into Agent tool foreground path
tasks.description: Modify the foreground blocking path in `src/tools/agent.ts` so that when a detach signal fires, the tool resolves immediately with a 'backgrounded' result. The sub-agent process must continue running, completion notification must be delivered later via CompletionDispatcher, and the active concurrency slot must only be released when the sub-agent actually finishes.
acceptance_criteria: Foreground Agent calls can be detached mid-execution via the detach signal
Detached sub-agent processes continue running uninterrupted
Tool returns a result with status 'backgrounded' and the sub-agent's agentId/alias
consumeCompletion() is NOT called for detached sub-agents (so CompletionDispatcher delivers the result later)
active.release() is deferred to handle.donePromise.finally() when detached, not released immediately
The detach scope is disposed in all code paths (normal completion, error, detach)
Background dispatch path (run_in_background=true) is unaffected
tasks.context: REQ-1, REQ-2, REQ-3, REQ-4, and REQ-7 require the Agent tool's foreground path to support mid-execution detach while maintaining correct concurrency tracking and enabling completion notifications.

tasks.title: Integrate detach into subagent_run tool across all modes
tasks.description: Modify `src/tools/run.ts` so all three execution modes (single, tasks, chain) support mid-execution detach. Single mode resolves immediately with a backgrounded result. Tasks mode returns completed results for finished sub-agents and backgrounded status for in-flight ones. Chain mode returns completed steps, backgrounds the current step, and marks remaining steps as abandoned. Concurrency slots are released only when each sub-agent actually finishes.
acceptance_criteria: Single-mode subagent_run can be detached, returning a 'backgrounded' tool result
Tasks-mode subagent_run detaches all in-flight tasks; completed tasks show normal results, in-flight ones show 'backgrounded'
Chain-mode subagent_run detaches the current step; completed steps show normal results, current shows 'backgrounded', remaining show 'abandoned'
consumeCompletion() is skipped for all detached sub-agents
active.release() is deferred for each detached sub-agent independently
The single shared detach scope is disposed after the tool returns
Non-detached completion (all tasks finish before user presses Ctrl+B) is unaffected
tasks.context: REQ-5 and REQ-6 define specific behavior for tasks and chain modes. REQ-7 requires correct concurrency tracking across all modes.

phases.name: Testing
phases.description: Unit tests covering the detach controller, tool integration, and TUI key handling

tasks.title: Unit tests for detach feature
tasks.description: Create `test/unit/detach.test.ts` covering: detach controller lifecycle (create scope, detachAll resolves promises, dispose cleans up, no-op when empty); Agent tool detach behavior (foreground detach returns backgrounded result, completion notification arrives later); subagent_run detach for single/tasks/chain modes (partial results, concurrency tracking); interrupt handler Ctrl+B integration (key consumed when scopes active, passed through when not). Follow existing test patterns in `test/unit/interrupt.test.ts` and `test/unit/concurrency.test.ts`.
acceptance_criteria: DetachController unit tests pass: createScope, detachAll, dispose, hasActiveScopes, multiple scopes
Agent tool detach test passes: foreground path returns backgrounded result when detach fires
subagent_run single mode detach test passes
subagent_run tasks mode detach test passes with mixed completed/backgrounded results
subagent_run chain mode detach test passes with completed/backgrounded/abandoned results
Interrupt handler Ctrl+B test passes: key consumed when scopes active, transparent when not
Concurrency tracking test passes: active counter correct after detach and after eventual completion
Completion notification delivery test passes: detached sub-agent completion triggers a CompletionDispatcher notification that injects into the parent conversation
All existing tests continue to pass
tasks.context: AC-1 through AC-7 require verifiable behavior. Tests validate correctness of the detach signal path, tool result formatting, concurrency invariants, and TUI key handling.
